ddls:
CREATE TABLE "religions" (
  "religion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each religion */,
  "religion_name" TEXT /* Name of the religion (e.g., Agnostic, Atheist, Buddhist) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description or notes about the religion */,
  "is_mainstream" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the religion is considered mainstream */,
  "total_followers" INTEGER /* An estimate of the total number of followers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("religion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "income_ranges" (
  "income_range_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income range */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Income range description (e.g., <$10k, $10-20k) */,
  "lower_bound" INTEGER /* The lower bound of the income range */,
  "upper_bound" INTEGER /* The upper bound of the income range */,
  "average_income" INTEGER /* The average income within this range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_range_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survey_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response record */,
  "religion_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the religion of the respondent */,
  "income_range_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the income range of the respondent */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Count of respondents in this category */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the individual respondent */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specific survey */,
  "is_unsure" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the respondent is unsure about their income */,
  "is_refused" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the respondent refused to disclose their income */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_religion_id FOREIGN KEY ("religion_id") REFERENCES religions ("religion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondent_demographics ("respondent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_income_range_id FOREIGN KEY ("income_range_id") REFERENCES income_ranges ("income_range_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "respondent_demographics" (
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each respondent */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group of the respondent (e.g., 18-24, 25-34) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the respondent (e.g., Male, Female, Other) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the respondent (e.g., City, State) */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* The highest level of education completed by the respondent */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* The marital status of the respondent */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* The employment status of the respondent */,
  "household_size" INTEGER /* The number of people in the respondent's household */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "researcher_name" TEXT /* Full name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the researcher (e.g., data analyst, project manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* The department or team the researcher belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* The level of access the researcher has to the data (e.g., basic, advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher accessing the data */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* The type of device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* The duration of the session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)
